title: Family Mentor
description:
1. Duties

i. Case Management

a) Conduct regular assessment of youth and caregivers using appropriate assessment tools

b) Develop youth-centric case plan for each youth identified according to the programme protocol for follow ups

c) Monitor and track attendance at schools and address any barriers to attendance (which may require engaging parents via home visits or other youth or parent engagement platforms)

d) Attend and convene case conferences, where necessary, with school teachers and mentors, MDT, and other agencies (e.g. Social Service Offices, Family Service Centres, etc.) to coordinate support for youth and their families at a systemic and case level (e.g. ComLink+)

e) Provide last mile delivery of support to families (e.g. accompanying parents and their youth for further health checks)

f) Support and ease transitions across different school stages, and withdrawals from the programme

g) Document case plans and notes of meetings/case conferences

h) Support the needs of other children between in the family if they are not known to any social services

ii.   Outreach

a) Organise, execute and participate in outreach activities to find and recruit potential disadvantaged families or families who need assistance together with volunteers. 

b) Record the outcomes of outreach to determine status of targeted youth.

iii.  Intake and Referral

a) Receive and process referrals from a variety of sources.

b) Seek consent from and determine the eligibility of families for programme components.

c) For families in the region who are not eligible for the programme, to refer them to relevant social service agencies.

d) Collate and maintain accurate and complete set of data on youth enrolled to the programme.

iv.  Engagement

a) Support the organisation mapping of community needs and assets.

b) Collate needs from families overseen by Tasek for possible sponsorship or donation opportunities.

c) Assist with disbursement and tracking of sponsored items to youth and families.

2. Job Requirements

a) Bachelor's degree in Social Work, Social Sciences or related field.

b) Keen to work with disadvantaged youth and families and willing to go the extra mile to support families in need

c) Good writing skills for case management andcomfortable with data collation for reporting purposes.

d) Strong organisational skills, with an ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines effectively.

e) Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with an ability to work collaboratively with various stakeholders including parents, caregivers, teachers, and community partners.

f) A passion for uplifting individuals and families in vulnerable or disadvantaged situations, which involves a keen interest in understanding and forming relationships with individuals in need of support while adhering to professional boundaries.

g) Proficiency in Microsoft Office, familiarity with assessment tools such as FAST, CANS and platforms such as Case Connect is an advantage.

h) Able to work on irregular hours due to home visit scheduling.

In addition, as a Tasek Academy and Social Services staff, you will gain:

● Meaningful Impact: Your work will directly contribute to improving the lives of children and youth in our community, providing them with opportunities for growth and development.

● Professional Development: Gain valuable experience and skills in programme management and mentoring within the social services sector.

● Collaborative Work Environment: Work within a supportive team that values collaboration, innovation, and holistic community development.

● Networking: You will have the chance to network with a team of passionate individuals and partner organisations, potentially opening doors for future collaborations or job opportunities.

● Creativity and Innovation: At Tasek Academy and Social Services, we welcome fresh ideas and perspectives. This is your chance to unleash your creativity to inspire and make a difference in our community.

If you are passionate about making a difference in the lives of disadvantaged youths, have experience in programme management and youth work, we would love to hear from you. Join us at Tasek Academy and Social Services, and let's make a positive impact in our community together.

postedCompany.description:
Tasek Academy and Social Services (the “Company”) is a non­profit organization incorporated on 14 December 2014. It is located at 1 Yung Sheng Road, #03­06 Jurong Town Community Club, Singapore 618495.

The mission of the Company is to support and help uplift the beneficiaries, the socially disadvantaged, who include, but are not limited to, ex­inmates, youth at risk, single parents, the needy and disabled and their families. Further, the Company has established the following objectives:­
(a) to develop innovative and/or customised solutions to support, uplift and assist its beneficiaries;
(b) to identify and create meaningful and fulfilling employment and other opportunities for our beneficiaries;
(c) to mentor and train our beneficiaries;
(d) the advancement of learning;
(e) the advancement of community development;
(f) the advancement of arts, heritage and culture;
(g) to provide grants, aid and/or assistance towards and/or in furtherance of any of the objects of the Company;
(h) to aid, assist and give relief in any manner and to any extent to any charitable institution whose object/s are similar to or comparable with the objects of the Company; and
(i) to carry out any other objects that are of a charitable, benevolent or philanthropic nature howsoever defined, as the Company may deem appropriate.
postedCompany.name: TASEK ACADEMY AND SOCIAL SERVICES LIMITED
